steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to compare the curviness of the two rivers. A successful answer must state which river is curvier — not just define sinuosity or describe one river alone.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘The high-sinuosity Luni River in India is curvier than the low-sinuosity Embarras River in Canada.’: reverses the data — it implies the Luni is the curvier river, but the Embarras has the higher sinuosity ratio. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘The Embarras River's ratio of stream length to valley length, also known as sinuosity, is greater than that of the Luni River.’: directly states that the Embarras River's sinuosity is greater than the Luni's — a clear, accurate comparison. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘By comparing the stream lengths of the Embarras and Luni rivers to their valley lengths, one can calculate their curviness, or sinuosity.’: only defines sinuosity without comparing the two rivers. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘In both the Embarras and the Luni rivers, curviness is determined by comparing stream length to valley length.’: only defines sinuosity without comparing the two rivers. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
